# Edward Anders

> DPhil candidate in Social Data Science at the Oxford Internet Institute,
> University of Oxford. Researching how AI-generated content shapes
> affective polarisation across democracies, using quantitative and
> computational methods to understand political behaviour.

This personal site is a single long-scroll page with four content sections
(About, Research, Projects, Contact) and a splash hero. The links below
point to each section anchor on the homepage.

## About

- [About — bio, education, experience](https://eawanders.com/#about): DPhil
  Social Data Science (Jesus College, Oxford, 2026–), MPhil Politics
  (St Antony's, Oxford, 2024–2026), BSc Economics and Politics (Bristol,
  2018–2021). Roles at Nuffield College (Graduate Researcher), Oxford
  Levant Advisory (Research Analyst), Complyance (Strategy Research
  Manager), Compose (Senior Founder's Associate).
- [Curriculum vitae (PDF)](https://eawanders.com/edward-anders-cv.pdf)

## Research

- [Research — focus, publications, grants](https://eawanders.com/#research)
- [MPhil thesis: AI Labels and Affective Polarisation (PDF, working paper, 2026)](https://eawanders.com/edward-anders-mphil-thesis.pdf):
  formal model of AI labels → trust → emotion → polarisation, tested
  with two YouGov-fielded UK survey experiments. Implications for the
  EU AI Act's content-disclosure requirements.
- "Talking To Machines: Synthetic Replication Games" — Duch, Batistoni
  et al. (Anders co-author, forthcoming, PNAS / Nature Computational
  Science in preparation). Platform for LLM-powered experimental trials
  in the social sciences, Nuffield College, Oxford.
- Grants: Oxford CESS Research Grant (£1,500, 2025); Norman Chester Fund
  Research Grant (£1,000, 2025); YouGov UniOM Survey Experiment Grant
  (×2, ~£1,000 each, 2024 + 2025).

## Projects

- [Projects](https://eawanders.com/#projects)
- [Simparlia](https://simparlia.com/): simulating parliamentarian voting
  behaviour through digital AI personas of MPs.
